main_event: Bullet-shaped metallic object with fin observed stationary at 10,000 ft over Mt. Kilimanjaro for 17 minutes before climbing rapidly east, by crew and passengers of East African Airways Lodestar.
sighted_object: bullet shaped with fin; dull silver colour; marked at regular intervals along fuselage with vertical dark bands; three definite stripes; over 200 ft. long; square-cut vertical fin at one end; absolutely stationary, then climbed rapidly eastward
witness_description: Crew and passengers of East African Airways Lodestar: 9 passengers and a crew of 2; Capt. J. Bicknell (pilot); radio officer Mr. D.W. Merrifield; master of the plane obtained signatures of all nine passengers confirming observation
interesting_points: Object stationary at approx 10,000 ft above Mt. Kilimanjaro for 17 minutes then climbed rapidly east reaching 40,000 ft before lost sight of
Capt. Bicknell calculated object covered 60 miles in 3 minutes of movement: speed nothing short of 1,000 m.p.h.
Left no vapour trail; had no visible means of propulsion
No windows or portholes, no engine mounts or jet pods, perfectly streamlined hull
Contact made with Nairobi re: French Constellation a/c from Eastleigh to Tannarive at approx 17,500 ft — could not be ruled in or out
Photos were taken but are not in file; motion picture film taken by passenger alleged to have disappeared
Master of plane obtained signatures of all nine passengers to document stating they had personally observed it
Page 9 contains unrelated entry: 19 Feb 1951 C-54 pilot green flare/rocket near Rodeo, N.M. at 9000 ft, concluded meteor (Project Twinkle report)
